title: Hymenasplenium furfuraceum
taxonomy: Hymenasplenium furfuraceum is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. It belongs to the genus Hymenasplenium, which is a group of ferns that are primarily found in Asia. The classification of Hymenasplenium furfuraceum is as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Asplenioids, family Aspleniaceae, genus Hymenasplenium, and species H. furfuraceum. This fern is a vascular plant with a rhizome base and fronds that are typically 10-30 cm in length. The classification of Hymenasplenium furfuraceum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns and its unique characteristics.
uses: Hymenasplenium furfuraceum has been used in traditional medicine, although its medicinal uses are not well-documented. intro: Hymenasplenium furfuraceum is a species of fern native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Aspleniaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases.
